footer.title: Jawafdehi
footer.subtitle: Nepal
footer.description: Nepal's permanent public archive of CIAA corruption cases — every filing, every document, forever.
footer.permanentArchive: Nepal's permanent public archive of CIAA corruption cases, free forever, built by Nepali volunteers.
footer.allDataPublicDomain: All data is in the public domain.
footer.disclaimer: The information provided on this website is for informative purposes only. We are not a judiciary body and do not have legal authority to prosecute or adjudicate cases. Our platform serves to document and raise awareness about corruption allegations and public accountability.

hero.title: Jawafdehi Nepal
hero.description: Documenting and tracking corruption cases to hold public entities accountable. Empowering Nepali citizens with transparency and verified information.

featuredCases.title: Featured Cases
featuredCases.description: Recent high-impact corruption cases under public scrutiny

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.description: Jawafdehi is an open-source civic‑tech platform for Nepal built by Nepali to document allegations of corruption. Grounded in a Gen‑Z mandate for transparency and action, we aim to become the Nepali accountability system—holding corrupt politicians and others accountable through independent, evidence‑first, and citizen‑powered means.

caseArchive.title: CIAA Case Archive
caseArchive.detail: We index every case the Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority files — including supporting documents, court orders, and legal filings, all in one place. Each record is structured so citizens, journalists, researchers, and volunteers can follow the full trail of a case: accused parties, alleged misconduct, locations, dates, official sources, and related documents.

plainLanguage.title: Plain-Language Summaries
plainLanguage.detail: Complex legal filings rewritten so any citizen can understand them — not just lawyers. Every summary is reviewed by human volunteers for factual accuracy before it is published. Summaries explain what happened, who is involved, what law or public duty is at issue, and where the case stands now.

aiResearch.title: AI Case Research
aiResearch.detail: Ask any question about a case or corruption trend in Nepali or English. Natural language queries against the full case archive will return instant, sourced answers. This research layer will help people compare cases, surface patterns across agencies or districts, and trace claims back to official documents.

cta.title: Know About a Case of Corruption?
cta.description: Help us hold public entities accountable. Report allegations of misconduct and contribute to transparency in governance.

hero.heading: How a <1>corruption</1> case goes from discovery to the <3>public archive</3>

hero.description: Corruption cases disappear from public consciousness. Officials retire, change portfolios, or simply wait for people to forget. We are building the infrastructure to make sure that never happens again.

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.paragraph1: Jawafdehi is dedicated to promoting transparency and accountability in Nepal by documenting, verifying, and publishing corruption cases involving public entities. We empower Nepali citizens with verified information to drive systemic change and hold public officials accountable for their actions.
mission.paragraph2: Through rigorous investigation, documentation, and public disclosure, we aim to create a more transparent governance system where corruption is exposed, justice is served, and public trust is restored.

permanentRecord.title: A permanent public record
permanentRecord.body: Every CIAA case we document stays in the archive indefinitely. We do not delete records. Officials retire, governments change, and news cycles move on — but the record remains, permanently accessible to any Nepali citizen.

factsNotOpinions.title: Facts, not opinions
factsNotOpinions.body: We present original documents, legal timelines, and verified facts. We simplify complex legal language so citizens can understand what happened — but we never editorialize. Every claim on this platform is traceable to a primary source.

humanReview.title: Human review of every AI-generated summary
humanReview.body: We use AI to process and summarize large volumes of legal documents faster. But every case summary generated by AI is reviewed by a human volunteer for factual accuracy before it is published. Speed does not come at the cost of accuracy.

publicDomain.title: All data in the public domain
publicDomain.body: The data we collect, clean, and structure is released into the public domain. Journalists, researchers, academics, and citizens are free to use it without restriction. Corruption knowledge should not be proprietary.

openSource.title: Open source technology
openSource.body: All of the software we build — from the case archive to the AI research tools — is open source and freely available. Any organization in any country can take our work and adapt it for their own accountability needs.

freeForever.title: Free, forever
freeForever.body: This platform will never charge for access. No subscriptions. No paywalls. No premium tiers. Public accountability infrastructure should be free to everyone who needs it.

volunteerPowered.title: Volunteer-powered, community-owned
volunteerPowered.body: Jawafdehi is built entirely by Nepali volunteers. We are not funded by government, political parties, or commercial interests. Our independence is structural — we have no institutional master to serve except the public.

techForCitizens.title: Technology in service of citizens
techForCitizens.body: Corruption-related records are scattered across dozens of government portals, court systems, and public databases — inaccessible to most citizens. We are building the technology to crowdsource and consolidate this knowledge into one permanent, publicly searchable archive. The goal is not a product. It is infrastructure.

cta.title: Volunteer with Jawafdehi
cta.description: Start by joining our Discord server or send us an email to introduce yourself and tell us which team interests you.

hero.description: Jawafdehi runs entirely on volunteer effort. Whether you are a technology enthusiast, a working professional, a legal expert, or a student, if you believe corruption should not be forgotten, there is a place for you here.

whoCanJoin.title: Who we're looking for
whoCanJoin.description: You don't need to be an expert. You need to care about Nepal and be willing to contribute your skills. Jawafdehi needs people who can help with technology, research, legal interpretation, data compilation, outreach, and translation.

globalCommunity.title: A worldwide network of Nepali professionals
globalCommunity.description: Our volunteers are based across Nepal and around the world — in the US, Thailand, India, and beyond. When you join Jawafdehi, you become part of a global community of Nepali professionals united by a single goal: making sure Nepal remembers.

technologyEnthusiasts.title: Technology Enthusiasts
technologyEnthusiasts.desc: Developers, data engineers, and AI practitioners who want to build civic infrastructure that matters. Frontend, backend, scraping, NLP — all skills are needed.

workingProfessionals.title: Working Professionals
workingProfessionals.desc: Accountants, analysts, policy professionals, and domain experts who can bring their field knowledge to understanding and verifying corruption cases.

legalProfessionals.title: Legal Professionals
legalProfessionals.desc: Lawyers, law students, and legal researchers who can help interpret CIAA filings, court orders, and legal timelines accurately for a public audience.

students.title: Students
students.desc: Students in law, public policy, computer science, journalism, or any field — this is real-world experience working on a problem that matters for Nepal.

teams.title: Our Volunteer Teams
teams.description: Each team owns a distinct part of the pipeline. Find the one that matches your skills.
dataScrapingResponsibilities: Archive government documents into digital text for large-scale analysis
Scrape Nepali media sources — existing and newly identified
Leverage AI to bootstrap case drafts and update them as new information emerges
Maintain the Nepal Entity Service data pipeline
outreachResponsibilities: Build relationships with investigative journalists, corruption watchdogs, and YouTubers
Source evidence and case leads from civil society organisations
Coordinate with CIAA, CIB, and media for primary source access
platformDevelopmentResponsibilities: Keep the platform accessible and performant for all stakeholders
Maintain open APIs for public data access
Build monitoring dashboards and run load testing
Ship website improvements based on user feedback
corruptionCompilationResponsibilities: Compile, structure, and publish corruption cases
Maintain case accuracy with ongoing updates and corrections
Ensure every published case meets verification standards
corruptionResearchResponsibilities: Investigate what legally and ethically constitutes corruption in each context
Analyse the role of corruption in Nepali governance structures
Evaluate the effectiveness of anti-corruption policy and institutional frameworks

hero.description: Every component we build is open source and free to use. The full stack is public on GitHub — built by Nepali volunteers and made available to anyone who wants to build on top of it.

Jawafdehi runs entirely on volunteer effort from Nepali citizens across technology, research, journalism, and public policy. No corporate interests. No government funding. Just people who believe accountability matters.

about.title: About Jawafdehi
about.subtitle: We are a civic tech initiative committed to transparency, accountability, and justice in Nepal.

hero.prefix: We are a civic tech initiative committed to

aboutUs.title: About Us
aboutUs.description: Jawafdehi is an open-source civic‑tech platform for Nepal built by Nepali to document allegations of corruption. Grounded in a Gen‑Z mandate for transparency and action, Jawafdehi aims to become the Nepali accountability system to bring an end to the practice of unaccountability—holding corrupt politicians, individuals, and others accountable through independent, evidence‑first, and citizen‑powered means. Using AI to process government documents, track news sources, and generate comprehensive investigation reports, Jawafdehi scales the fight against corruption through technology.

building.title: What We're Building
building.description1: Corruption-related records are scattered across dozens of government portals, court systems, and public databases — inaccessible to most citizens. We are building the technology and the volunteer network to bring it all into one permanent, publicly searchable knowledge base.
building.description2: Free to use. Open source. Built entirely by Nepali volunteers who believe accountability has no expiry date.

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.description1: Jawafdehi is dedicated to promoting transparency and accountability in Nepal by documenting, verifying, and publishing corruption cases involving public entities. We empower Nepali citizens with verified information to drive systemic change and hold public officials accountable for their actions.
mission.description2: Through rigorous investigation, documentation, and public disclosure, we aim to create a more transparent governance system where corruption is exposed, justice is served, and public trust is restored.

values.title: Our Core Values
values.description: The principles that guide how we document, verify, and serve the public.

integrity.title: Integrity
integrity.description: We document public-interest cases with fairness, care, and ethical responsibility.

transparency.title: Transparency
transparency.description: We make records, sources, and case information open and accessible.

accuracy.title: Accuracy
accuracy.description: Every case is grounded in verifiable public documents and cited sources.

publicService.title: Public Service
publicService.description: We build for citizens, researchers, journalists, and communities seeking accountability.

lbn.title: Let's Build Nepal (LBN)
lbn.description: A civic tech organization focused on building technology solutions for social good in Nepal. LBN brings technical expertise and development resources to the platform.

document.label: Document Corruption Cases:
document.description: We systematically collect and organize information about corruption cases involving public entities.

verify.label: Verify Information:
verify.description: All cases undergo rigorous verification through multiple independent sources before publication.

track.label: Track Progress:
track.description: We monitor the status of each case and update the public on investigations and outcomes.

empower.label: Empower Citizens:
empower.description: We provide accessible, searchable information to help citizens make informed decisions and demand accountability.

advocate.label: Advocate for Change:
advocate.description: We use data and documented cases to advocate for policy reforms and stronger anti-corruption measures.

information.title: Understanding Corruption & Our Process
information.subtitle: Learn how we verify information, what constitutes corruption, and how you can contribute to transparency.

whatIsCorruption.title: What is Corruption?
whatIsCorruption.description: Corruption is the abuse of entrusted power for private gain. In the context of public service, it represents a betrayal of the public trust and includes various forms of misconduct.

bribery.title: Bribery
bribery.description: Offering, giving, receiving, or soliciting something of value to influence the actions of an official.

embezzlement.title: Embezzlement
embezzlement.description: Theft or misappropriation of funds placed in one's trust or belonging to one's employer.

nepotism.title: Nepotism & Favoritism
nepotism.description: Favoritism granted to relatives or friends regardless of merit, especially in hiring or contract awards.

conflictOfInterest.title: Conflict of Interest
conflictOfInterest.description: Situations where personal interests improperly influence professional duties and decisions.

abuseOfResources.title: Abuse of Public Resources
abuseOfResources.description: Misuse of public funds, assets, or authority for personal benefit or unauthorized purposes.

verification.title: How We Verify Information
verification.description: We maintain rigorous standards for verification to ensure accuracy and reliability. Every case undergoes a multi-stage review process before publication.

sourceCollection.title: 1. Source Collection
sourceCollection.description: We gather information from government records, media reports, NGO investigations, public documents, and credible whistleblowers.

crossVerification.title: 2. Cross-Verification
crossVerification.description: Multiple independent sources are required. Claims must be corroborated through official documents, witness accounts, or verifiable evidence.

teamReview.title: 3. Team Review
teamReview.description: Our moderation team reviews each case for accuracy, legal compliance, and ethical standards before approval.

continuousUpdates.title: 4. Continuous Updates
continuousUpdates.description: Cases are monitored for developments. Updates, responses from accused entities, and investigation outcomes are added as they emerge.

standards.title: Verification Standards
standards.minSources: Minimum of two independent, credible sources for each allegation
standards.officialDocs: Official documentation or verifiable evidence whenever possible
standards.clearDistinction: Clear distinction between verified facts, allegations, and ongoing investigations
standards.rightToRespond: Right to respond granted to all accused entities
standards.protectSources: Protection of sources and sensitive information

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.description: We compiled a list of answers to address your most pressing questions regarding Jawafdehi.

howToReport.question: How can I report a corruption case?
howToReport.answer1: You can report a case through our "Report Allegation" form. Please provide as much detail and supporting documentation as possible. You can choose to submit anonymously or with your identity.
howToReport.answer2: All submissions are reviewed by our moderation team before being published. We may contact you for additional verification if needed.

afterSubmit.question: What happens after I submit a report?
afterSubmit.answer: Your submission enters our verification queue. Our team will review the information, verify through multiple sources, and determine if it meets our publication standards. This process typically takes 7-14 days depending on case complexity. You'll be notified of the outcome if you provided contact information.

anonymous.question: Is my identity protected if I report anonymously?
anonymous.answer: Yes. We take contributor privacy seriously. Anonymous submissions do not store any identifying information, and we have measures to protect all contributors from retaliation. Personal data like phone numbers and national ID numbers are never published.

entityResponse.question: Can accused entities respond to allegations?
entityResponse.answer: Absolutely. We believe in fair representation. For now, our team contacts accused individuals and organizations directly through credible, verifiable sources before publishing any response alongside the allegations.

inaccurate.question: What if information on the platform is inaccurate?
inaccurate.answer: Despite our rigorous verification process, errors can occur. If you find inaccurate information, please contact us immediately through our feedback form with supporting evidence. We will review and correct any verified inaccuracies promptly. All corrections are documented in the case's audit trail.

funding.question: How is this platform funded?
funding.answer: Jawafdehi is funded through partnerships with civic organizations and potential grants from transparency-focused foundations. We maintain editorial independence and do not accept funding from government entities or organizations that could compromise our integrity.

useInfo.question: Can I use information from this platform?
useInfo.answer: Yes! All information on this platform is publicly accessible and can be freely used for research, journalism, advocacy, or educational purposes. We encourage proper attribution and responsible use of the data.

report.title: Report an Allegation
report.description: Help maintain accountability by reporting allegations of misconduct. All submissions are reviewed by our moderation team before publication.
report.entityNamePlaceholder: Name of the individual or organization
report.allegationTitlePlaceholder: Brief, descriptive title of the allegation
report.detailedDescriptionPlaceholder: Provide a detailed description of the allegation, including dates, locations, and circumstances
report.sourcesPlaceholder: Provide links to news articles, official documents, or other credible sources (one per line)
report.evidenceUpload: Click to upload or drag and drop
report.contributorInfoDesc: Provide your contact details if you'd like us to follow up
report.importantNoticeText: All submissions are reviewed by our moderation team for accuracy and credibility. False allegations may have legal consequences. Please ensure your report is factual and evidence-based.
report.termsAgreement: I confirm that the information provided is accurate to the best of my knowledge and I agree to the platform's terms of use

submitted.title: Report Submitted
submitted.description: Your allegation has been submitted for moderation review. Thank you for contributing to public accountability.

templateDownload.instructions: Complete the template with as much detail as possible, then send it by email or WhatsApp:
